cap_three_allows_three_rare_split.description: Raising the cap to three allows an eight-alternative split of C-valid, E/F-noisy bodies.

cap_two_refuses_three_rare_split.description: The same three-rare-body evidence must refuse selection under the default cap.

duplicate_suffix_label_no_source_cut.description: Rare body shares the suffix label, so the upstream duplicate-label guard should block per-body alternatives.

overlapping_body_labels_no_source_cut.description: Dominant and rare length-2 bodies overlap labels, so upstream duplicate-label checks block the cut.

partial_signal_final_not_used.description: Final probes would identify E as noise, but validation does not, so the selector must stay unresolved.

split_count_two_length2_mixed_final_gene.description: Configured count-two rare bodies can be split even when rare bodies have length two.

split_length2_mixed_final_generalization.description: Length-2 rare body EG is valid while HJ is noise.

split_mixed_width_final_generalization.description: A length-2 rare body is valid while a singleton rare body is noise.

split_singleton_drop_both_final_precisio.description: Validation rejects both rare bodies; final probes test unseen rare-only and mixed rare negatives.

split_singleton_mixed_final_generalizati.description: Validation keeps C and drops E; final probes check unseen C-only repeats and E-containing negatives.

training_negative_conflict_protocol.description: A validation negative that appears in training must block selection.

validation_final_overlap_flagged.description: The harness must flag validation/final reuse even when selection succeeds.

wrong_rare_count_no_candidates.description: With rare_body_count=1, a count-two rare body should not generate per-body alternatives.
